codeReview.summary: The feature adds broad cross-area coverage in a new integration test file, but VAL-CROSS-008 restart/deploy assertions are not validated against a real restart boundary. The tests explicitly reuse the same in-memory RelayMessageStore instance across server re-creation, so they can pass even if actual process restart/deploy loses lifecycle state.

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-008 tests simulate restart by reusing the same in-memory messageStore (see `Shared message store survives "restart"` and subsequent `server = await createServer()` calls). This does not test true restart/deploy integrity because process-memory loss and store rehydration paths are never exercised.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation contract language for VAL-CROSS-008 implies restart/deploy integrity, but current coverage is a same-process simulation over shared memory. This limitation should be explicitly captured in shared state so future workers/validators do not over-interpret this evidence as process-level persistence validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:219-221 defines restart/deploy integrity; test/cross/golden-path.test.ts:1302 states it 'simulates persistent storage' via shared message store.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires manual-check rationale when manual CLI/API/SSE checks are skipped, but the handoff marks followedProcedure=true without interactiveChecks or an explicit skip rationale. Skill guidance may need clearer handling for test-only features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:31-33; handoff 2026-02-28T17-17-44-274Z...json lines 128-131 show followedProcedure=true with empty deviations and no interactiveChecks entry under verification.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking coverage gap: restart/deploy integrity is only simulated with a shared in-memory store, not validated across a true restart boundary.
